description: Implement git worktree lifecycle management in internal/worktree/. All operations shell out to git via os/exec. Create, remove, list worktrees. Check cleanliness and get commits. All git commands must capture stderr for error reporting and use exec.CommandContext for timeout support. Reference docs/implementation-plan.md Milestone 4 for all method signatures and the Worktree/Commit structs.
commit: feat(worktree): add git worktree lifecycle management
steps: Create internal/worktree/worktree.go with Manager struct (repoRoot, worktreeBase, branchPrefix) and Worktree/Commit structs
Implement Create(ctx, taskID, baseRef) creating branch {prefix}{taskID} at {worktreeBase}/{taskID} via git worktree add, returning Worktree with BaseSHA from git rev-parse
Implement Remove(ctx, taskID) running git worktree remove --force then git branch -D
Implement List(ctx) parsing git worktree list --porcelain output
Implement IsClean(ctx, path) checking git status --porcelain is empty
Implement GetCommits(ctx, path, baseSHA) parsing git log --oneline {baseSHA}..HEAD

Create internal/worktree/worktree_test.go using t.TempDir() with real git repos: test create verifies directory+branch exist, test two independent worktrees, test remove cleans up, test List, test IsClean on clean and dirty worktrees, test GetCommits
acceptance_criteria: Create produces a worktree at the expected path with correct branch name
Create returns the base SHA from git rev-parse
Remove deletes both the worktree directory and the branch
IsClean returns true for clean worktrees, false when files are modified or untracked
GetCommits returns commits between base SHA and HEAD
All git errors include stderr output in the error message
All commands use exec.CommandContext for timeout support
All tests pass with real git operations (no mocks)
